sections_cited: section 34A of the Federal Excise Act

chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Spl. Federal Excise Reference Application No.90 of 2021 Date Order with Signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For order on CMA No.3160/2024 2. For hearing of main case. 06.03.2026 None present This matter is pending since 2021 without any progress. On 10.09.2024 following order was passed: “None present on behalf of the Applicant nor any intimation received. Matter is coming- up for non-prosecution as to office objections. As a last and final chance, seven days’ time is granted for compliance failing which this Reference Application will be dismissed for non-prosecution. To come-up on 18.09.2024.” On 18.09.2024 following order was passed: “1. As a last and final chance, seven days’ further time is allowed, however, if compliance is not made within such time, this Reference Application shall stand dismissed without any further order.” Even today, the applicant remained unrepresented without any intimation and justification. Therefore, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, this reference is dismissed for non- prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 34A of the Federal Excise Act, 2005. JUDGE JUDGE Asif
